_comment: Robinson piece definitions. Edges and angles in PhiBase. Vertices listed CCW. The 'apex' is the vertex with the unique angle (36 for T, 108 for G).
_format: kind: { vertices: [{label, angle_deg}, ...], edges: [{from, to, length_phibase: [n, p]}, ...] }

_orientations.comment: Each piece can be placed in 10 distinct orientations (rotations by 36 degrees), and mirrored. The WFC enumerates over kind * rotation * mirror when computing legalPieces at an open edge. Snap to the edge by matching one of the piece's three edges to the open edge (length AND scale must match).

_attachment_protocol.step_1: Given open edge e with endpoints v0, v1 and length L (in PhiBase): enumerate piece edges with matching length L.
_attachment_protocol.step_2: For each matching piece-edge, two rotational placements per side of e (left/right of CCW direction). Reject if the side is already occupied.
_attachment_protocol.step_3: For each remaining placement, check legality: candidate brings angle_at_v0 and angle_at_v1; both must extend the existing vertex words legally per vertexWords.json rules.
_attachment_protocol.step_4: Check geometric non-overlap with already-placed pieces (exact in PhiBase).
_attachment_protocol.step_5: Return the surviving placements as the superposition for edge e.
